items.title: Parallelize independent work
items.example: `await Promise.all([fetchUser(id), fetchPrefs(id), fetchHistory(id)])` — three independent calls run together; latency = the slowest one, not the sum.

items.title: Always handle rejections
items.avoid: Bare awaits with no error handling.
items.example:
```ts
process.on('unhandledRejection', e => { console.error('unhandled:', e); process.exit(1) })
// + try/catch around every await that could throw
```

items.title: Don't drop Promises
items.avoid: Calling an async function without awaiting it.
items.prefer: await or deliberately manage the returned Promise.
items.why: Avoids race conditions and unobserved errors.
items.example: Enable ESLint `no-floating-promises` — flags any `asyncFn()` whose return isn't awaited, returned, or `.catch`'d.

items.title: Pick the right combinator
items.example: Fanning out to N flaky embeddings APIs and you want everything that worked → `Promise.allSettled`. Choosing the first responding mirror → `Promise.any`. Timing out a single call → `Promise.race` with a `setTimeout` rejection.
